tasks.title: Create multi-stage Dockerfile with base tooling
tasks.description: Build Dockerfile using node:22-bookworm base image with multi-stage build for Go compilation and comprehensive development environment setup
tasks.details:
Multi-stage Dockerfile structure:

Stage 1 (Go builder):
- FROM golang:1.22-bookworm AS go-builder
- WORKDIR /build
- Clone cc-connect repository
- Build cc-connect binary: go build -ldflags='-w -s' -o /build/cc-connect

Stage 2 (Final):
- FROM node:22-bookworm
- USER root (for installation, will switch to node:1000 via gosu in entrypoint)

System packages (apt):
- tmux (terminal multiplexer)
- gosu (user switching with correct signal forwarding)
- python3 python3-pip python3-venv
- golang (for user access)
- cron (for scheduled tasks)
- git curl wget build-essential
- Install GitHub CLI (gh) from official apt repository
- Install Tailscale from official apt repository

Python environment:
- pip3 install --break-system-packages: uv pandas matplotlib seaborn scipy

Rust toolchain (as node user):
- Switch to node user, install rustup non-interactive
- rustup-init -y --default-toolchain stable
- Install to /home/node/.cargo

Node.js global packages (npm install -g):
- @anthropic-ai/claude-code
- @openai/codex
- @google/gemini-cli
- opencode-ai
- task-master-ai
- ralph-orchestrator (from GitHub source if needed)
- ccman
- uipro-cli

Build-time skill preparation:
- Clone https://github.com/obra/superpowers to /home/node/.superpowers
- Clone https://github.com/openclaw/skills to /home/node/.openclaw-skills (for data-analyst skill)

Copy artifacts:
- COPY --from=go-builder /build/cc-connect /usr/local/bin/
- COPY entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh (RUN chmod +x)
- COPY user-init.sh.example /home/node/user-init.sh.example

Final setup:
- Set ownership: chown -R node:node /home/node
- ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
- CMD ["bash"]
tasks.testStrategy:
Build verification:
- docker build --no-cache -t coding-agent:test .
- Verify build completes without errors
- Check layer count (should use multi-stage efficiently)
- Verify Go toolchain not in final image: docker history coding-agent:test | grep -i golang (should be empty)
- Test all CLIs available: docker run --rm coding-agent:test bash -c "claude --version && codex --version && gemini --version && opencode --version && task-master --version && ccman --help && cc-connect --version && gh --version && tmux -V && tailscale --version"
- Verify Python deps: docker run --rm coding-agent:test python3 -c "import pandas, matplotlib, seaborn, scipy; print('OK')"
- Verify Rust: docker run --rm coding-agent:test bash -c "rustup --version && cargo --version"
- Check file ownership: docker run --rm coding-agent:test ls -la /home/node (should be node:node)

tasks.title: Implement entrypoint.sh with root→node switching
tasks.description: Create comprehensive entrypoint script that runs as root for initialization, then switches to node user via gosu for main process execution
tasks.details:
entrypoint.sh implementation:

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Must run as root initially
if [ "$(id -u)" != "0" ]; then
  echo "[entrypoint] ERROR: must run as root" >&2
  exit 1
fi

DATA_ROOT="${DATA_ROOT:-/data/coding-agent}"

# Step 1: Create and fix ownership of all mount target directories
mkdir -p \
  /home/node/.claude \
  /home/node/.codex \
  /home/node/.config/gemini \
  /home/node/.config/opencode \
  /home/node/.task-master \
  /home/node/.config/gh \
  /var/lib/tailscale \
  /home/node/.cargo \
  /var/spool/cron/crontabs \
  /home/node/projects \
  /home/node/openclaw \
  /home/node/workspace-1 \
  /home/node/workspace-2 \
  /home/node/workspace-3

chown -R node:node /home/node/
chown -R node:node /var/spool/cron/crontabs

# Step 2: Start background services
echo "[entrypoint] starting cron..."
service cron start

echo "[entrypoint] starting tailscaled..."
tailscaled --state=/var/lib/tailscale/tailscaled.state --socket=/var/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock &
sleep 2

if [ -n "${TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY}" ]; then
  echo "[entrypoint] connecting to Tailscale..."
  tailscale up --authkey="${TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY}" --hostname="${TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME:-coding-agent}"
fi

# Step 3: Generate tool config files (idempotent)
# Claude Code settings.json
CLAUDE_SETTINGS="/home/node/.claude/settings.json"
if [ ! -f "${CLAUDE_SETTINGS}" ] && [ -n "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}" ]; then
  cat > "${CLAUDE_SETTINGS}" <<EOF
{
  "apiKey": "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}"
  $([ -n "${ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL}" ] && echo ", \"baseURL\": \"${ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL}\"")
}
EOF
  chown node:node "${CLAUDE_SETTINGS}"
  echo "[entrypoint] generated: ${CLAUDE_SETTINGS}"
fi

# Codex config.toml
CODEX_CONFIG="/home/node/.codex/config.toml"
if [ ! -f "${CODEX_CONFIG}" ] && [ -n "${OPENAI_API_KEY}" ]; then
  cat > "${CODEX_CONFIG}" <<EOF
[auth]
api_key = "${OPENAI_API_KEY}"
$([ -n "${OPENAI_BASE_URL}" ] && echo "base_url = \"${OPENAI_BASE_URL}\"")

[model]
default = "${CODEX_MODEL:-gpt-5-codex}"
wire_api = "responses"
EOF
  chown node:node "${CODEX_CONFIG}"
  echo "[entrypoint] generated: ${CODEX_CONFIG}"
fi

# Gemini config.json
GEMINI_CONFIG="/home/node/.config/gemini/config.json"
if [ ! -f "${GEMINI_CONFIG}" ] && [ -n "${GEMINI_API_KEY}" ]; then
  mkdir -p "$(dirname ${GEMINI_CONFIG})"
  cat > "${GEMINI_CONFIG}" <<EOF
{
  "apiKey": "${GEMINI_API_KEY}"
}
EOF
  chown -R node:node /home/node/.config/gemini
  echo "[entrypoint] generated: ${GEMINI_CONFIG}"
fi

# Task Master global.env
TASKMASTER_ENV="/home/node/.task-master/global.env"
if [ ! -f "${TASKMASTER_ENV}" ]; then
  mkdir -p "$(dirname ${TASKMASTER_ENV})"
  cat > "${TASKMASTER_ENV}" <<EOF
$([ -n "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}" ] && echo "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}")
$([ -n "${OPENAI_API_KEY}" ] && echo "OPENAI_API_KEY=${OPENAI_API_KEY}")
$([ -n "${OPENROUTER_API_KEY}" ] && echo "OPENROUTER_API_KEY=${OPENROUTER_API_KEY}")
$([ -n "${PERPLEXITY_API_KEY}" ] && echo "PERPLEXITY_API_KEY=${PERPLEXITY_API_KEY}")
$([ -n "${OPENAI_BASE_URL}" ] && echo "OPENAI_BASE_URL=${OPENAI_BASE_URL}")
TASKMASTER_MAIN_PROVIDER=${TASKMASTER_MAIN_PROVIDER:-anthropic}
TASKMASTER_MAIN_MODEL=${TASKMASTER_MAIN_MODEL:-claude-sonnet-4-20250514}
$([ -n "${TASKMASTER_RESEARCH_PROVIDER}" ] && echo "TASKMASTER_RESEARCH_PROVIDER=${TASKMASTER_RESEARCH_PROVIDER}")
$([ -n "${TASKMASTER_RESEARCH_MODEL}" ] && echo "TASKMASTER_RESEARCH_MODEL=${TASKMASTER_RESEARCH_MODEL}")
$([ -n "${TASKMASTER_FALLBACK_PROVIDER}" ] && echo "TASKMASTER_FALLBACK_PROVIDER=${TASKMASTER_FALLBACK_PROVIDER}")
$([ -n "${TASKMASTER_FALLBACK_MODEL}" ] && echo "TASKMASTER_FALLBACK_MODEL=${TASKMASTER_FALLBACK_MODEL}")
EOF
  chown -R node:node /home/node/.task-master
  echo "[entrypoint] generated: ${TASKMASTER_ENV}"
fi

# Step 4: Install skills (idempotent - check if already exists)
if [ ! -d "/home/node/.claude/skills/planning-with-files" ]; then
  echo "[entrypoint] installing planning-with-files skill..."
  su - node -c "npx skills add OthmanAdi/planning-with-files --all -y"
fi

if [ ! -d "/home/node/.claude/skills/data-analyst" ]; then
  echo "[entrypoint] installing data-analyst skill..."
  cp -r /home/node/.openclaw-skills/data-analyst /home/node/.claude/skills/
  cp -r /home/node/.openclaw-skills/data-analyst /home/node/.codex/skills/
  chown -R node:node /home/node/.claude/skills/data-analyst
  chown -R node:node /home/node/.codex/skills/data-analyst
fi

if [ ! -d "/home/node/.claude/skills/codex" ]; then
  echo "[entrypoint] installing oil-oil/codex skill..."
  su - node -c "npx skills add oil-oil/codex -a claude-code -y"
fi

# ui-ux-pro-max for non-Claude tools
if [ ! -d "/home/node/.codex/extensions/ui-ux-pro-max" ]; then
  echo "[entrypoint] installing ui-ux-pro-max for Codex/Gemini..."
  su - node -c "uipro init --ai codex --offline || true"
  su - node -c "uipro init --ai gemini --offline || true"
fi

# Step 5: Execute user-init.sh hook if exists
USER_INIT="${DATA_ROOT}/user-init.sh"
if [ -f "${USER_INIT}" ]; then
  echo "[entrypoint] running user-init.sh..."
  bash "${USER_INIT}"
fi

# Step 6: Switch to node user and exec main process
echo "[entrypoint] switching to node user..."
exec gosu node "$@"
tasks.testStrategy:
Functional testing:
- Start container with test env vars and verify all config files generated
- Check idempotency: restart container, verify configs not overwritten (add marker to config file, restart, check marker persists)
- Verify cron running: docker compose exec coding-agent pgrep cron
- Verify tailscaled running: docker compose exec coding-agent pgrep tailscaled
- Verify final process runs as node: docker compose exec coding-agent whoami (should output 'node')
- Test Claude Code refuses root: docker compose exec -u root coding-agent claude --version (should error)
- Test user-init.sh execution: create test script in /data/coding-agent/user-init.sh that touches /tmp/test-marker, restart container, verify /tmp/test-marker exists
- Verify skills installed: ls /data/coding-agent/config/claude/skills/ (should contain planning-with-files, data-analyst, codex)

tasks.title: Create docker-compose.yml with three-layer volume architecture
tasks.description: Implement production docker-compose.yml with comprehensive volume bindings following the three-layer mental model (auto config layer, project layer, optional extra mounts)
tasks.details:
docker-compose.yml implementation:

version: '3.8'

services:
  coding-agent:
    image: ${DOCKER_IMAGE:-your-dockerhub-username/coding-agent:latest}
    container_name: ${CONTAINER_NAME:-coding-agent}
    restart: unless-stopped
    
    environment:
      - TZ=${TZ:-Asia/Shanghai}
      - NODE_ENV=${NODE_ENV:-development}
      - DATA_ROOT=${DATA_ROOT:-/data/coding-agent}
      
      # Claude Code
      - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}
      - ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=${ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL}
      
      # Codex
      - OPENAI_API_KEY=${OPENAI_API_KEY}
      - OPENAI_BASE_URL=${OPENAI_BASE_URL}
      - CODEX_MODEL=${CODEX_MODEL:-gpt-5-codex}
      
      # Gemini
      - GEMINI_API_KEY=${GEMINI_API_KEY}
      
      # Task Master
      - OPENROUTER_API_KEY=${OPENROUTER_API_KEY}
      - PERPLEXITY_API_KEY=${PERPLEXITY_API_KEY}
      - TASKMASTER_MAIN_PROVIDER=${TASKMASTER_MAIN_PROVIDER:-anthropic}
      - TASKMASTER_MAIN_MODEL=${TASKMASTER_MAIN_MODEL:-claude-sonnet-4-20250514}
      - TASKMASTER_RESEARCH_PROVIDER=${TASKMASTER_RESEARCH_PROVIDER}
      - TASKMASTER_RESEARCH_MODEL=${TASKMASTER_RESEARCH_MODEL}
      - TASKMASTER_FALLBACK_PROVIDER=${TASKMASTER_FALLBACK_PROVIDER}
      - TASKMASTER_FALLBACK_MODEL=${TASKMASTER_FALLBACK_MODEL}
      
      # GitHub CLI
      - GH_TOKEN=${GH_TOKEN}
      
      # Tailscale
      - TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY=${TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY}
      - TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME=${TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME:-coding-agent}
    
    volumes:
      # ── Layer 1: Auto config (user doesn't need to understand) ──
      - ${DATA_ROOT:-/data/coding-agent}/config/claude:/home/node/.claude
      - ${DATA_ROOT:-/data/coding-agent}/config/codex:/home/node/.codex
      - ${DATA_ROOT:-/data/coding-agent}/config/gemini:/home/node/.config/gemini
      - ${DATA_ROOT:-/data/coding-agent}/config/opencode:/home/node/.config/opencode
      - ${DATA_ROOT:-/data/coding-agent}/config/taskmaster:/home/node/.task-master
      - ${DATA_ROOT:-/data/coding-agent}/config/gh:/home/node/.config/gh
      - ${DATA_ROOT:-/data/coding-agent}/config/tailscale:/var/lib/tailscale
      - ${DATA_ROOT:-/data/coding-agent}/config/cargo:/home/node/.cargo
      - ${DATA_ROOT:-/data/coding-agent}/cron/crontabs:/var/spool/cron/crontabs
      
      # ── Layer 2: Project directory ──
      - ${DATA_ROOT:-/data/coding-agent}/projects:/home/node/projects
      
      # ── Layer 3: Optional extra mounts (empty = /dev/null placeholder) ──
      - ${MOUNT_OPENCLAW:-/dev/null}:/home/node/openclaw
      - ${MOUNT_EXTRA_1:-/dev/null}:/home/node/workspace-1
      - ${MOUNT_EXTRA_2:-/dev/null}:/home/node/workspace-2
      - ${MOUNT_EXTRA_3:-/dev/null}:/home/node/workspace-3
    
    ports:
      - "${PORT_CC_CONNECT:-8080}:8080"   # cc-connect
      - "${PORT_RALPH:-3000}:3000"        # ralph-orchestrator
      - "${PORT_DEV:-9000}:9000"          # development/misc
    
    cap_add:
      - NET_ADMIN  # Required for Tailscale
    
    devices:
      - /dev/net/tun:/dev/net/tun  # Required for Tailscale
    
    stdin_open: true
    tty: true

Also create docker-compose.dev.yml for local development:

version: '3.8'

services:
  coding-agent:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    image: coding-agent:dev
    # Inherits all other settings from main docker-compose.yml
    extends:
      file: docker-compose.yml
      service: coding-agent
tasks.testStrategy:
Volume and mount verification:
- docker compose up -d
- Verify all Layer 1 directories auto-created on host: ls /data/coding-agent/config/
- Test Layer 2 bidirectional sync: echo "host-test" > /data/coding-agent/projects/test.txt && docker compose exec coding-agent cat ~/projects/test.txt
- Test Layer 3 MOUNT_OPENCLAW: mkdir -p /data/openclaw && echo "openclaw" > /data/openclaw/test.txt, set MOUNT_OPENCLAW=/data/openclaw in .env, restart, verify accessible in container
- Test empty Layer 3 doesn't break: leave MOUNT_EXTRA_1/2/3 empty, verify container starts successfully
- Verify Tailscale capabilities: docker inspect coding-agent | grep -A5 CapAdd (should show NET_ADMIN)
- Test port mappings: curl localhost:8080, localhost:3000, localhost:9000 (should connect even if no service running)
- Rebuild test: docker compose down && docker compose build --no-cache && docker compose up -d, verify data persists

tasks.title: Create comprehensive .env template
tasks.description: Generate complete .env.example file with all configuration options, clear comments explaining three-layer model, and sensible defaults
tasks.details:
.env.example content structure:

# ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
# ║              Coding Agent — Environment Configuration            ║
# ║  Copy this file to .env and fill in your settings                ║
# ║  This file contains sensitive information - DO NOT commit to git ║
# ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

# ── Paths ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Root directory for all persistent data on host (modify requires container rebuild)
DATA_ROOT=/data/coding-agent

# ── Container Basics ──────────────────────────────────────────────
CONTAINER_NAME=coding-agent
TZ=Asia/Shanghai
NODE_ENV=development

# ── Claude Code ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-xxxxxxxxxx
# ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://your-proxy.example.com

# ── Codex ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-xxxxxxxxxx
# OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://your-proxy.example.com/v1   # Must support Responses API
# CODEX_MODEL=gpt-5-codex

# ── Gemini CLI ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GEMINI_API_KEY=AIzaxxxxxxxxxx

# ── Task Master ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OPENROUTER_API_KEY=sk-or-xxxxxxxxxx    # Recommended: unified multi-model proxy
# PERPLEXITY_API_KEY=pplx-xxxxxxxxxx     # Recommended for research model
TASKMASTER_MAIN_PROVIDER=anthropic
TASKMASTER_MAIN_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-20250514
# TASKMASTER_RESEARCH_PROVIDER=perplexity
# TASKMASTER_RESEARCH_MODEL=sonar-pro
# TASKMASTER_FALLBACK_PROVIDER=anthropic
# TASKMASTER_FALLBACK_MODEL=claude-haiku-4-5-20251001

# ── GitHub CLI ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# GH_TOKEN=ghp_xxxxxxxxxx

# ── Tailscale ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY=tskey-auth-xxxxxxxxxx
# TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME=coding-agent

# ── Port Mappings ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
PORT_CC_CONNECT=8080
PORT_RALPH=3000
PORT_DEV=9000

# ── Layer 3: Optional Extra Mounts ────────────────────────────────
# Fill in host directory paths as needed, leave empty to skip mounting
#
# Example: Mount OpenClaw directory
# When OpenClaw container is down, access its files here for debugging/repair
# MOUNT_OPENCLAW=/data/openclaw
MOUNT_OPENCLAW=

# Other directories you want to bring in (any purpose)
# MOUNT_EXTRA_1=/home/user/my-other-project
# MOUNT_EXTRA_2=/mnt/nas/datasets
# MOUNT_EXTRA_3=
MOUNT_EXTRA_1=
MOUNT_EXTRA_2=
MOUNT_EXTRA_3=

# ── Docker Image ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# For production: pull from Docker Hub
# For development: use docker-compose.dev.yml which builds locally
DOCKER_IMAGE=your-dockerhub-username/coding-agent:latest

Include inline documentation explaining:
- Three-layer mental model in comments
- Which settings are required vs optional
- Format examples for API keys
- When to use base URLs (proxy scenarios)
- Tailscale requirements (NET_ADMIN capability)
tasks.testStrategy:
Configuration validation:
- Copy .env.example to .env
- Fill in minimal required keys (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY)
- docker compose config (should parse without errors)
- Verify all variables interpolate correctly: docker compose config | grep -A5 environment
- Test with custom DATA_ROOT: change to /tmp/test-coding-agent, docker compose up -d, verify directories created at new location
- Test with custom ports: change PORT_CC_CONNECT=18080, verify port mapping correct in docker compose ps
- Test optional variables: leave MOUNT_* empty, TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY empty, verify container starts
- Git safety check: verify .env in .gitignore, attempt git add .env (should be ignored)

tasks.title: Create user-init.sh.example template
tasks.description: Develop comprehensive user initialization hook example file with common software installation patterns and clear usage instructions
tasks.details:
user-init.sh.example implementation:

#!/bin/bash
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# user-init.sh — User Custom Initialization Script
#
# Purpose: Install personalized software that you need.
#          This script runs on every container start,
#          automatically reinstalls after rebuild.
#
# Usage:
#   cp /data/coding-agent/user-init.sh.example \
#      /data/coding-agent/user-init.sh
#   Then edit this file and uncomment what you need.
#
# Note: Running as root, no need for sudo.
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ── System Tools ───────────────────────────────────────────────
# apt-get update -qq
# apt-get install -y -q vim tree htop jq ripgrep fd-find

# ── Node.js Global Packages ────────────────────────────────────
# npm install -g typescript ts-node eslint prettier
# npm install -g @nestjs/cli create-react-app vite

# ── Python Packages ────────────────────────────────────────────
# pip3 install jupyterlab notebook --break-system-packages
# pip3 install requests beautifulsoup4 scrapy --break-system-packages

# ── Rust Tools (install as node user to preserve in ~/.cargo) ──
# su - node -c "cargo install ripgrep bat exa fd-find"
# su - node -c "cargo install tokei cargo-watch"

# ── Custom Shell Configuration ─────────────────────────────────
# cat >> /home/node/.bashrc << 'EOF'
# # Custom aliases
# alias ll='ls -alF'
# alias la='ls -A'
# alias l='ls -CF'
# alias ..='cd ..'
# 
# # Environment variables
# export EDITOR=vim
# export VISUAL=vim
# 
# # Custom prompt
# PS1='\[\033[01;32m\]\u@\h\[\033[00m\]:\[\033[01;34m\]\w\[\033[00m\]\$ '
# EOF

# ── Database Clients ───────────────────────────────────────────
# apt-get install -y -q postgresql-client mysql-client redis-tools

# ── Docker CLI (to access host Docker daemon if socket mounted) ─
# apt-get install -y -q docker.io
# usermod -aG docker node

# ── Language-specific Version Managers ─────────────────────────
# # nvm for Node.js version management
# su - node -c "curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.0/install.sh | bash"
# 
# # pyenv for Python version management
# apt-get install -y -q make build-essential libssl-dev zlib1g-dev \
#   libbz2-dev libreadline-dev libsqlite3-dev wget curl llvm \
#   libncursesw5-dev xz-utils tk-dev libxml2-dev libxmlsec1-dev libffi-dev liblzma-dev
# su - node -c "curl https://pyenv.run | bash"

# ── Cloud CLI Tools ────────────────────────────────────────────
# # AWS CLI
# curl "https://awscli.amazonaws.com/awscli-exe-linux-x86_64.zip" -o "awscliv2.zip"
# unzip awscliv2.zip && ./aws/install && rm -rf aws awscliv2.zip
# 
# # Google Cloud SDK
# echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/cloud.google.gpg] https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt cloud-sdk main" | \
#   tee -a /etc/apt/sources.list.d/google-cloud-sdk.list
# curl https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | \
#   apt-key --keyring /usr/share/keyrings/cloud.google.gpg add -
# apt-get update && apt-get install -y google-cloud-sdk

# ── Development Databases (for local testing) ──────────────────
# # Install and configure PostgreSQL locally
# apt-get install -y -q postgresql postgresql-contrib
# service postgresql start
# su - postgres -c "createuser -s node"

echo "[user-init] Custom initialization complete."

Provide clear categories:
- System utilities
- Language package managers
- Custom shell configs
- Database clients
- Cloud tools
- Local services

Include idempotency notes where relevant
tasks.testStrategy:
Template and execution testing:
- Verify file bundled in Docker image at /home/node/user-init.sh.example
- Copy to persistent location: docker compose exec coding-agent cp /home/node/user-init.sh.example /data/coding-agent/user-init.sh
- Uncomment vim installation section in /data/coding-agent/user-init.sh
- Restart container: docker compose restart coding-agent
- Verify vim installed: docker compose exec coding-agent which vim (should output /usr/bin/vim)
- Test idempotency: restart again, verify no errors from re-running apt install
- Test custom .bashrc: add alias to user-init.sh, restart, docker compose exec coding-agent bash -c "alias ll" (should show custom alias)
- Rebuild test: docker compose down && docker compose build --no-cache && docker compose up -d, verify user software reinstalled automatically

tasks.title: Write comprehensive README.md for end users
tasks.description: Create user-friendly documentation explaining three-layer architecture, quick start guide, and common operations without requiring Docker expertise
tasks.details:
README.md structure:

# Coding Agent Docker Distribution

An all-in-one containerized coding agent toolkit with Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Task Master, and more.

## What is This?

A production-ready Docker container that bundles multiple AI coding assistants with persistent configuration, project data, and optional directory mounting.

## Three-Layer Mental Model

Understand data persistence in three simple concepts:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Layer 1: /data/coding-agent/                               │
│  "The container's brain - auth tokens and configs live here"│
│  "Don't touch, won't lose on rebuild"                       │
│  → Auto-mounted, user does nothing                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Layer 2: Project Directory                                  │
│  "My code is here, safe to write, won't lose on rebuild"    │
│  → Default: /data/coding-agent/projects/ → ~/projects/      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Layer 3: Optional Extra Directories                         │
│  "Want to bring in other stuff like OpenClaw? Fill .env"    │
│  → MOUNT_OPENCLAW, MOUNT_EXTRA_1/2/3, empty = skip         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Quick Start (5 Steps)

1. **Clone repository**
   ```bash
   git clone <repo-url>
   cd coding-agent
   ```

2. **Copy environment template**
   ```bash
   cp .env.example .env
   ```

3. **Fill in API Keys**
   Edit `.env` and add your keys:
   ```bash
   ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-xxx
   OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-xxx
   GEMINI_API_KEY=AIza-xxx
   ```

4. **Start container**
   ```bash
   docker compose up -d
   ```

5. **Enter and use**
   ```bash
   docker compose exec coding-agent bash
   claude  # or codex, gemini, task-master, etc.
   ```

## Mounting OpenClaw Directory

When your OpenClaw container has issues and you need direct file access:

1. Edit `.env`:
   ```bash
   MOUNT_OPENCLAW=/data/openclaw
   ```

2. Restart container:
   ```bash
   docker compose restart coding-agent
   ```

3. Access inside container:
   ```bash
   docker compose exec coding-agent bash
   cd ~/openclaw
   # Debug, repair, or recover OpenClaw data
   ```

## Making Your Software Survive Rebuilds

Install once, available forever using the user initialization hook:

1. **Copy example file**
   ```bash
   docker compose exec coding-agent cp /home/node/user-init.sh.example /data/coding-agent/user-init.sh
   ```

2. **Edit on host** (persists across rebuilds)
   ```bash
   vim /data/coding-agent/user-init.sh
   # Uncomment software you need (vim, htop, custom npm packages, etc.)
   ```

3. **Restart to apply**
   ```bash
   docker compose restart coding-agent
   ```

4. **Rebuild anytime** - your software auto-reinstalls
   ```bash
   docker compose down
   docker compose build --no-cache
   docker compose up -d
   # All your custom software is back!
   ```

## Manual Skills Installation (One-Time)

Some skills require interactive installation but persist forever:

### Superpowers Skill

**Claude Code:**
```bash
docker compose exec coding-agent bash
claude
# In Claude session:
/plugin marketplace add obra/superpowers-marketplace
/plugin install superpowers@superpowers-marketplace
```

**Codex:**
In Codex session, tell agent to fetch and follow `.codex/INSTALL.md`

**Gemini:**
```bash
gemini extensions install https://github.com/obra/superpowers
```

### UI-UX-Pro-Max Skill (Claude Code)
```bash
claude
/plugin marketplace add nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill
/plugin install ui-ux-pro-max@ui-ux-pro-max-skill
```

## Tools Included

### AI Coding Agents
- Claude Code (`claude`)
- Codex (`codex`)
- Gemini CLI (`gemini`)
- OpenCode (`opencode`)
- Task Master (`task-master`)
- Ralph Orchestrator (`ralph`)
- ccman (`ccman`)
- cc-connect (`cc-connect`)

### Development Environment
- Node.js 22 + npm
- Python 3 + pip + uv
- Go
- Rust + Cargo
- GitHub CLI (`gh`)

### Session Management
- tmux (persist sessions across disconnects)
- Tailscale (zero-config VPN)

### Pre-installed Skills
- planning-with-files
- data-analyst
- oil-oil/codex
- ui-ux-pro-max (Codex/Gemini/OpenCode)

## Common Operations

### Using tmux (Prevent Task Interruption)
```bash
docker compose exec coding-agent bash
tmux new -s main          # Create session
claude                    # Start long-running task
# Ctrl+B then D           # Detach (task keeps running)
docker compose exec coding-agent tmux attach -t main  # Reattach later
```

### Tailscale Connection
```bash
# Auto-connect on start (set in .env):
TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY=tskey-auth-xxx

# Manual connection:
docker compose exec coding-agent tailscale up
docker compose exec coding-agent tailscale status
```

### GitHub CLI Auth
```bash
docker compose exec coding-agent gh auth login
# Follow prompts - auth persists in /data/coding-agent/config/gh/
```

## FAQ

**Q: Auth tokens expired, how to reset?**
A: Delete specific config dir and restart:
```bash
rm -rf /data/coding-agent/config/claude/settings.json
docker compose restart coding-agent
# Re-authenticate when prompted
```

**Q: How to completely reset everything?**
A: Delete data root (WARNING: loses all auth, projects, configs):
```bash
docker compose down
rm -rf /data/coding-agent
docker compose up -d
# Fresh start, re-configure everything
```

**Q: How to use cron for scheduled tasks?**
A: Edit crontab inside container (persists in /data/coding-agent/cron/):
```bash
docker compose exec coding-agent crontab -e
# Add your schedule, e.g.:
# 0 2 * * * /home/node/scripts/backup.sh
```

**Q: Can I use multiple extra mounts beyond 3?**
A: Yes, edit `docker-compose.yml` volumes section:
```yaml
volumes:
  - /host/path/to/extra4:/home/node/workspace-4
```

**Q: Container won't start with Tailscale errors?**
A: Your host may not support NET_ADMIN capability. Comment out in docker-compose.yml:
```yaml
# cap_add:
#   - NET_ADMIN
# devices:
#   - /dev/net/tun:/dev/net/tun
```

## Architecture

- **Base Image:** node:22-bookworm
- **User:** node (uid=1000)
- **Data Root:** /data/coding-agent/ (configurable via DATA_ROOT)
- **Persistent Volumes:** 9 config dirs + projects + optional mounts
- **Network:** Tailscale mesh VPN (optional)
- **Init System:** entrypoint.sh (root→node via gosu)

## License

[Specify your license]

## Contributing

[Contribution guidelines]

tasks.title: Implement GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow
tasks.description: Create automated multi-architecture (amd64/arm64) Docker image build and push pipeline triggered on main branch pushes and version tags
tasks.details:
GitHub Actions workflow file: .github/workflows/build-push.yml

Implementation:

name: Build & Push Multi-Arch Image

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    tags:
      - 'v*.*.*'

env:
  IMAGE: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}/coding-agent

jobs:
  build-push:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Docker meta
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.IMAGE }}
          tags: |
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}
            type=sha,prefix=main-,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=semver,pattern={{major}}.{{minor}}
            type=semver,pattern={{major}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v') }}

      - name: Set up QEMU
        uses: docker/setup-qemu-action@v3

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Login to Docker Hub
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}
          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build-push
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          echo "### Build Complete ✅" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "**Image:** \`${{ env.IMAGE }}\`" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "**Tags:** ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
          echo "**Digest:** \`${{ steps.build-push.outputs.digest }}\`" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

Trigger Strategy:
- Push to main → tags: latest, main-<short-sha>
- Push tag v1.2.3 → tags: v1.2.3, 1.2, 1, latest

GitHub Secrets Required:
- DOCKERHUB_USERNAME: Docker Hub username
- DOCKERHUB_TOKEN: Docker Hub access token (not password)

Documentation to add:
- Setup instructions in README or CONTRIBUTING.md
- How to configure secrets
- Expected build times (15-60min depending on cache)
- Tag strategy explanation

Optional enhancements:
- Add build status badge to README
- Send notifications on build failure
- Add security scanning with Trivy
tasks.testStrategy:
CI/CD pipeline testing:
- Create test repository with workflow file
- Configure DOCKERHUB_USERNAME and DOCKERHUB_TOKEN secrets
- Test push to main: verify latest and main-<sha> tags created
- Test version tag: git tag v0.1.0 && git push --tags, verify semver tags (v0.1.0, 0.1, 0, latest)
- Verify multi-arch manifest: docker manifest inspect <image>:latest (should show amd64 and arm64)
- Test pull on different architectures: docker pull on x86_64 and ARM64 Mac, verify correct variant pulled
- Check GHA cache: verify subsequent builds faster than first build
- Test failure scenario: introduce syntax error in Dockerfile, verify build fails and doesn't push
- Verify build summary appears in GitHub Actions UI
- Test tag overwrite: push new version tag, verify latest moves to new version

tasks.title: Create comprehensive test suite (regression tests)
tasks.description: Implement all test cases from PRD Section 14 covering build, persistence, user management, tools, mounts, configuration, and services
tasks.details:
Test suite implementation using bash scripts organized by category:

Create tests/ directory with:
- tests/tc-build.sh (TC-BUILD-01 through 04)
- tests/tc-user.sh (TC-USER-01 through 02)
- tests/tc-tool.sh (TC-TOOL-01)
- tests/tc-persist.sh (TC-PERSIST-01 through 05)
- tests/tc-mount.sh (TC-MOUNT-01 through 03)
- tests/tc-userinit.sh (TC-USERINIT-01 through 03)
- tests/tc-env.sh (TC-ENV-01 through 03)
- tests/tc-cron.sh (TC-CRON-01 through 02)
- tests/tc-tmux.sh (TC-TMUX-01 through 02)
- tests/tc-tailscale.sh (TC-TAILSCALE-01 through 03)
- tests/tc-skills.sh (TC-SKILLS-01 through 04)
- tests/tc-port.sh (TC-PORT-01 through 02)
- tests/tc-release.sh (TC-RELEASE-01 through 02)
- tests/run-all.sh (orchestrator)

Example test structure (tc-build.sh):

#!/bin/bash
set -e

source tests/lib/helpers.sh

test_build_01() {
  echo "[TC-BUILD-01] No-cache build succeeds"
  docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml build --no-cache
  assert_exit_code 0 "Build should succeed"
}

test_build_02() {
  echo "[TC-BUILD-02] Go toolchain not in final image"
  result=$(docker history coding-agent:latest | grep -i golang || true)
  assert_empty "$result" "Go should not be in final image"
}

test_build_03() {
  echo "[TC-BUILD-03] Python dependencies pre-installed"
  docker run --rm coding-agent:latest python3 -c "import pandas, matplotlib, seaborn, scipy; print('OK')"
  assert_output_contains "OK"
}

test_build_04() {
  echo "[TC-BUILD-04] All dev tools available"
  docker run --rm coding-agent:latest bash -c "
    python3 --version && pip3 --version && uv --version &&
    go version && rustup --version && cargo --version &&
    node --version && npm --version && tmux -V && tailscale --version"
  assert_exit_code 0 "All tools should be available"
}

# Run all tests
test_build_01
test_build_02
test_build_03
test_build_04

Helper library (tests/lib/helpers.sh):

#!/bin/bash

assert_exit_code() {
  local expected=$1
  local message=$2
  if [ $? -ne $expected ]; then
    echo "❌ FAIL: $message (exit code $?)" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "✅ PASS: $message"
}

assert_empty() {
  local value=$1
  local message=$2
  if [ -n "$value" ]; then
    echo "❌ FAIL: $message (got: $value)" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "✅ PASS: $message"
}

assert_output_contains() {
  local expected=$1
  # Implementation for output checking
}

Main orchestrator (tests/run-all.sh):

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== Coding Agent Test Suite ==="
echo ""

./tests/tc-build.sh
./tests/tc-user.sh
./tests/tc-tool.sh
./tests/tc-persist.sh
./tests/tc-mount.sh
./tests/tc-userinit.sh
./tests/tc-env.sh
./tests/tc-cron.sh
./tests/tc-tmux.sh
./tests/tc-tailscale.sh
./tests/tc-skills.sh
./tests/tc-port.sh
./tests/tc-release.sh

echo ""
echo "=== All Tests Passed ✅ ==="

Integrate into CI:
Add to GitHub Actions workflow to run tests before push
tasks.testStrategy:

- Run tests in CI pipeline
- Verify test output is readable and informative
- Check test execution time (should complete within 10 minutes for full suite)
- Verify cleanup: tests should not leave orphaned containers/volumes

CONTRIBUTING.md content:

# Contributing to Coding Agent

## Development Setup

1. Clone repository
2. Copy .env.example to .env and fill in test API keys
3. Use docker-compose.dev.yml for local development:
   ```bash
   docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml build
   docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d
   ```

## Making Changes

1. Create feature branch: `git checkout -b feature/your-feature`
2. Make changes to Dockerfile, entrypoint.sh, or compose files
3. Test locally: `./tests/run-all.sh`
4. Commit with descriptive message
5. Push and create pull request

## Testing

- Run full test suite: `./tests/run-all.sh`
- Run specific test category: `./tests/tc-build.sh`
- Add tests for new features in appropriate tests/tc-*.sh file

## Updating Skills

- Build-time skills: modify Dockerfile
- Entrypoint skills: modify entrypoint.sh
- Update README.md manual skills section as needed

## Versioning

We follow semantic versioning:
- MAJOR: Breaking changes (config format changes, volume structure changes)
- MINOR: New features (new tools, new skills, new capabilities)
- PATCH: Bug fixes, documentation updates

To release:
1. Update version in relevant files
2. Commit: `git commit -m "Release v1.5.0"`
3. Tag: `git tag v1.5.0`
4. Push: `git push && git push --tags`
5. GitHub Actions will build and push automatically

## Code Style

- Shell scripts: Use shellcheck for validation
- Bash: 2-space indentation
- Comments: Explain why, not what
- Error handling: Always check exit codes for critical operations

## Pull Request Guidelines

- Include test coverage for changes
- Update README.md if user-facing changes
- Update CHANGELOG.md with notable changes
- Ensure CI passes before requesting review

Also create:
- CHANGELOG.md (version history)
- SECURITY.md (security policy and vulnerability reporting)
- CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (if open source)

tasks.title: Create release documentation and Docker Hub description
tasks.description: Prepare Docker Hub repository description, release notes template, and upgrade guide for production deployment
tasks.details:
Docker Hub Description (appears on Docker Hub repository page):

# Coding Agent - All-in-One AI Development Container

A production-ready Docker container bundling multiple AI coding assistants with persistent configuration and intelligent multi-layer data management.

## Quick Start

```bash
# 1. Create environment file
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/<org>/coding-agent/main/.env.example
mv .env.example .env

# 2. Edit .env and add your API keys
vim .env

# 3. Create docker-compose.yml
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/<org>/coding-agent/main/docker-compose.yml

# 4. Start container
docker compose up -d

# 5. Use your tools
docker compose exec coding-agent bash
claude  # or codex, gemini, task-master, etc.
```

## What's Included

### AI Coding Agents
- Claude Code
- Codex
- Gemini CLI
- OpenCode
- Task Master
- Ralph Orchestrator
- ccman & cc-connect

### Development Environment
- Node.js 22
- Python 3 + uv
- Go
- Rust + Cargo
- GitHub CLI

### Infrastructure
- tmux (session persistence)
- Tailscale (mesh VPN)
- cron (scheduled tasks)

### Pre-installed Skills
- planning-with-files
- data-analyst
- ui-ux-pro-max

## Data Persistence

Three-layer architecture ensures you never lose data:
1. **Config layer**: Auth tokens, tool configs (auto-managed)
2. **Project layer**: Your code and workspaces
3. **Optional mounts**: Bring in external directories as needed

## Documentation

- Full Guide: https://github.com/<org>/coding-agent/blob/main/README.md
- API Keys Setup: See .env.example
- Skills Installation: See README

## Support

- Issues: https://github.com/<org>/coding-agent/issues
- Discussions: https://github.com/<org>/coding-agent/discussions

## Tags

- `latest`: Latest stable release
- `v1.5.0`, `1.5`, `1`: Semantic version tags
- `main-<sha>`: Development builds from main branch

## License

[Specify license]

Release Notes Template (RELEASE_NOTES.md):

# Release Notes

## v1.5.0 (YYYY-MM-DD)

### 🎉 New Features
- Initial public release
- Multi-architecture support (amd64, arm64)
- Three-layer persistent data architecture
- User initialization hook system
- 8 AI coding agents pre-installed

### 🛠 Tools Included
- Claude Code CLI
- Codex
- Gemini CLI
- OpenCode
- Task Master
- Ralph Orchestrator
- cc-connect
- GitHub CLI

### 📦 Development Environment
- Node.js 22
- Python 3.11 + uv
- Go 1.22
- Rust (latest stable)

### 🔧 Infrastructure
- tmux for session management
- Tailscale for secure networking
- Cron for scheduled tasks

### 📚 Skills Pre-installed
- planning-with-files (Claude/Codex/Gemini/OpenCode)
- data-analyst (Claude/Codex)
- oil-oil/codex (Claude)
- ui-ux-pro-max (Codex/Gemini/OpenCode)

### 🐛 Known Issues
- Tailscale requires NET_ADMIN capability (may not work on some PaaS)
- Codex requires Responses API support (some proxies incompatible)
- First-time Rust build adds ~500MB to image size

### 📖 Documentation
- Comprehensive README with quick start
- user-init.sh.example for custom software
- Full test suite with 40+ regression tests

### 🚀 Upgrade Notes

First release - no upgrade needed.

For future upgrades:
```bash
docker compose pull
docker compose down
docker compose up -d
```
All data in /data/coding-agent persists automatically.

Upgrade Guide (UPGRADING.md):

# Upgrade Guide

## General Upgrade Process

```bash
# 1. Pull latest image
docker compose pull

# 2. Stop current container
docker compose down

# 3. Start with new image
docker compose up -d
```

Your data in `/data/coding-agent` is preserved automatically.

## Version-Specific Notes

### Upgrading to v2.0 (Future)
Breaking changes will be documented here.

### Upgrading to v1.5 (Current)
Initial release - no previous version to upgrade from.

## Rollback Procedure

If issues occur after upgrade:

```bash
# Specify previous version in .env
DOCKER_IMAGE=your-username/coding-agent:v1.4.0

# Restart with old version
docker compose down
docker compose up -d
```

## Data Migration

No data migration needed for minor/patch versions.
Major version upgrades with data schema changes will include migration scripts.

## Testing After Upgrade

```bash
# Verify all tools work
docker compose exec coding-agent bash -c "claude --version && codex --version"

# Check data persistence
ls /data/coding-agent/config/

# Run health checks
./tests/run-all.sh
```
